steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what role the Algeria sentence plays in the passage. The Algeria sentence describes a shift away from solid fuels. The text then introduces Heltberg's Ghana study to show the energy ladder model is reductive — fuel choice depends on more than income.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'shared cause': Claims Algeria and Ghana share the same cause. The passage never identifies a shared cause. ✗
  • Choice B — 'illustrates the kind of phenomenon... inadequately accounted for': Algeria shows a real-world fuel-switching trend that the energy ladder model is then said to handle poorly. ✓
  • Choice C — 'evaluating the model': Reframes Algeria as an evaluation of the model, but the passage uses Algeria as an example, not a test. ✗
  • Choice D — 'two competing explanations': Only one explanation (the ladder) is called inadequate; no second explanation is offered. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
